text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okonkwo, Renata
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ORDERING PROVIDER: [Oncology Attending]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Right level II cervical lymph node, core needle biopsy (18-gauge, ultrasound-guided, 3 cores)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
55-year-old female with known Stage IV melanoma (BRAF V600E-mutant, primary mid-back skin, T4bN3cM1c). Currently on dabrafenib plus trametinib. New right cervical lymphadenopathy identified on urgent restaging imaging in the setting of rising LDH and new right upper quadrant discomfort. Prior pathologic diagnosis of metastatic melanoma confirmed at initial staging (right axillary lymph node core biopsy and subcutaneous abdominal wall nodule). Biopsy submitted for histologic confirmation of progression and concurrent liquid biopsy sent for ctDNA resistance profiling (Guardant360, results pending separately).

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "right level II cervical lymph node core biopsy." Three tan-pink cylindrical core biopsy fragments, each measuring approximately 1.1-1.3 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. Aggregate tissue volume approximately 0.04 mL. Cores demonstrate focal areas of soft, grayish-white tissue interspersed with more fibrous tan stroma. No grossly necrotic or hemorrhagic material identified, though one core contains a small focus of pale gray tissue. All three cores are submitted in toto in one cassette.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Sections from all three cores are examined. The lymph node architecture is largely effaced and replaced by sheets and nests of highly atypical epithelioid and spindled cells with markedly enlarged nuclei, prominent macronucleoli (so-called "owl-eye" nucleoli), and abundant pale to eosinophilic cytoplasm. Intranuclear pseudoinclusions are focally present. Tumor cells are arranged in solid nests and loosely cohesive sheets; occasional cells contain fine brown cytoplasmic melanin pigment. Stroma is desmoplastic in foci, with intermixed lymphoid cells at the periphery of the biopsy cores. Mitotic figures are readily identified and numerous, with some atypical forms; estimated mitotic rate is elevated (consistent with IHC Ki-67 data, see below). Focal geographic necrosis is present in one core, occupying approximately 15-20% of the sampled tissue. No organoid or glandular architecture is identified. No carcinomatous, lymphomatous, or other non-melanocytic morphology is seen.

The cytomorphology and growth pattern are consistent with and morphologically similar to the prior diagnostic biopsy of right axillary lymph node (Accession SP-XXXX-XXXXX [prior accession]), supporting metastatic melanoma as the diagnosis. No significant maturation, regression, or treatment-related fibrosis of the type seen with complete or near-complete response is identified in the sampled tissue; the viable tumor component is predominant.

---

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES:

The following immunostains were performed using validated antibodies and appropriate positive and negative controls. Results are as follows:

  SOX10:         Diffuse strong nuclear positivity in tumor cells (3+)
  S100 protein:  Diffuse strong cytoplasmic and nuclear positivity in tumor cells (3+)
  HMB-45:        Moderate to strong granular cytoplasmic positivity, patchy distribution (2-3+)
  Melan-A (MART-1): Moderate cytoplasmic positivity in approximately 70% of tumor cells (2+)
  MiTF:          Positive (nuclear, moderate, ~80% of cells)
  Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 55% of tumor cell nuclei positive (markedly elevated)
  PD-L1 (22C3 clone, TPS): 15% (1+, focal membranous staining on tumor cells)
  AE1/AE3 (pan-cytokeratin): Negative
  CD45 (LCA):    Negative in tumor cells; positive in residual admixed lymphocytes (internal control satisfactory)
  CD20:          Negative in tumor cells
  CD3:           Negative in tumor cells; positive in scattered T-lymphocytes (internal control)
  Synaptophysin: Negative
  Chromogranin:  Negative

Comment: The Ki-67 proliferative index of approximately 55% represents an increase compared to the initial diagnostic specimen (Ki-67 ~45%), consistent with more aggressive tumor biology at progression. PD-L1 TPS of 15% is concordant with prior testing.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

FISH for BRAF amplification: Pending (reflexed per oncology request; concurrent ctDNA liquid biopsy submitted for resistance profiling, results to be reported separately).

Molecular/NGS: Not performed on this specimen; concurrent circulating tumor DNA analysis (Guardant360) submitted from peripheral blood draw obtained at time of biopsy procedure.

---

DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT LEVEL II CERVICAL LYMPH NODE, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   - METASTATIC MALIGNANT MELANOMA
   - Morphologically consistent with and immunophenotypically identical to prior documented primary and metastatic disease
   - Ki-67 proliferative index approximately 55% (increased from prior)
   - PD-L1 TPS: 15% (22C3)
   - Focal necrosis present
   - See comment

COMMENT: The histologic, cytomorphologic, and immunohistochemical profile of this core needle biopsy is consistent with metastatic melanoma and is concordant with the patient's known history and prior pathologic specimens. The elevated Ki-67 (55%) compared to the initial diagnostic excision (45%) and the identification of focal necrosis are in keeping with an actively proliferating metastatic deposit. No morphologic features of treatment-related regression are identified. Clinical correlation with concurrent ctDNA analysis (Guardant360) and FISH results (when available) is recommended for comprehensive resistance profiling in the setting of clinical progression on BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy.

---

DICTATED BY: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY
FINAL REPORT — SIGNED
